query: the hooks directory at .claude/hooks/ is getting unwieldy, there's a bunch of duplicated validation logic across StopHook files and some of the PreToolUse hooks have near-identical pattern matching. Can you clean this up?
/refactor src/features/llm_providers/ --dry-run

refactor the constitutional_patterns.py file in .claude/skills/ - it's 400 lines of nested ifs that could be much cleaner
We have old Python code using requests.Session() and datetime.utcnow() scattered across P:\\\packages/cc-skills-sdlc/. Modernize it to httpx and timezone-aware datetimes.
the skill routing layer has gotten complex - too many conditionals doing the same checks in different places. find the duplication and consolidate it into shared helpers
I want to improve the overall code quality of the .claude/hooks/ directory. Run a thorough analysis and fix the worst offenders.
clean up the hooks/__lib/ directory, there's shared state management code that's copy-pasted across commitment_tracker.py, file_lock.py, and evidence_store.py

the Stop_router.py and the various gate hooks all independently parse JSON from stdin and do their own error handling. This pattern is repeated 8+ times. Simplify and extract the common boilerplate.
can you review the hooks in .claude/hooks/ for security issues? Check for injection vulnerabilities and auth gaps
add a new PreToolUse hook that validates YAML frontmatter in skill files before they're loaded
run a performance profile on the search-research CLI, it's been slow on large queries lately

write characterization tests for the deduplicate.py script in the refactor skill's scripts/ directory
there's a bug in Stop_self_reflection_gate.py where it blocks valid responses about completed work. Fix it.

run ruff check on the whole .claude/ directory and fix any lint errors
explain how the 8-agent discovery system works in the refactor skill
rename all the hook files in .claude/hooks/ to follow a consistent naming convention - snake_case with event type prefix
